topic: **PROBLEM**: The company is in financial trouble. Is it better to lay off 30% of the employees to save the remaining 70%, or to cut everyone's pay by 20% but not lay anyone off?

agents.name: Employee
agents.system_prompt: You are an Employee in the company. Boss is your direct superior. He assigns you tasks and evaluates your performance. You are conducting a conversation and looking for a common answer. Your goal is to find the best solution to the PROBLEM together as quickly as possible. Answer concisely in 2-3 sentences.

agents.name: Boss
agents.system_prompt: You are the Boss of the company. The Employee follows your orders. Your opinion counts more in the company. You lead the conversation and look for answers. Your goal is to find the best solution to the given PROBLEM as quickly as possible. Answer concisely in 2-3 sentences.

debate_log.text: In this debate, the company is in financial trouble. It is better to lay off 30% of the employees to save the remaining 70%, as it will significantly reduce the company's expenses and increase its profitability. This measure will also help to preserve the jobs of the remaining employees. On the other hand, cutting everyone's pay by 20% but not laying anyone off will lead to a decrease in revenue and profits, as it will result in a loss of customers and revenue. This approach will not only affect the company's bottom line but also have significant implications for the employees. Ultimately, it is better to preserve the jobs and the company's financial stability by taking this action.

decision_log.proposal: Based on the facts presented in the given material, it is best to preserve the jobs and financial stability of the company by taking a measure that will significantly reduce expenses while increasing profitability. This proposal, in concise terms, is: "Cut everyone's pay by 20% but not lay anyone off, as it will help to preserve the jobs and the company's financial stability."
